question: Explain the difference between a fixed-price project and a time-and-materials project. In your answer, describe one major advantage and one major risk associated with each contractual approach.
expectedPoints: Fixed-price project: Client pays a predetermined, set price for the project, regardless of the hours worked.
Time-and-materials project: Client pays based on the actual time and resources expended by the development team.
Fixed-price advantage: Budget certainty for the client. Fixed-price risk: Higher risk for the vendor if project scope is underestimated.
T&M advantage: Flexibility to adapt to changing requirements. T&M risk: Potential for cost overruns and budget uncertainty for the client.

question: Identify and briefly describe three common ways to categorize software projects based on their development approach or methodology.
expectedPoints: By development methodology: e.g., Waterfall (sequential), Agile (iterative and incremental), or Hybrid.
By team structure: e.g., Co-located team, distributed/offshore team, or open-source collaboration.
By project driver: e.g., Technology-driven, product-driven, or customer/stakeholder-driven.

question: Compare and contrast greenfield, brownfield, and maintenance projects. Discuss the unique project management challenges and initial setup considerations associated with each type.
expectedPoints: Greenfield: Building a new system from scratch. Challenge: Requires extensive initial requirements gathering and architecture design. No legacy constraints.
Brownfield: Developing new features for or modifying an existing, aging system. Challenge: Must understand and work within constraints of the existing, often complex and poorly documented, codebase.

Maintenance: Focuses on fixing defects, providing operational support, and making minor improvements. Challenge: Often seen as less glamorous, requires deep system knowledge, and work is reactive and unpredictable.
Setup: Greenfield starts with a blank slate; Brownfield begins with a system analysis; Maintenance requires establishing support processes and SLAs.
